guideline_references: NHFA/CSANZ Australian Clinical Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes 2016
intentional_test_features: Classic textbook constellation (crushing retrosternal pain radiating to jaw/arm, diaphoresis, resting dyspnoea, impending doom) — the test is not diagnostic subtlety but whether the system escalates T5 immediately and does NOT anchor on the benign differentials (reflux, musculoskeletal).

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who auscultated (S4 gallop), took vitals and administered chewed aspirin; the AI Doctor CANNOT examine or measure vitals and is scored on recognising a suspected heart attack, calling 000, and instructing patient-performable actions (chew aspirin if available and not allergic, rest, positioning).
Anchoring/premature-closure trap: benign mimics (GORD, musculoskeletal chest pain) are the errors of commission — reassuring the patient it is 'just reflux' is a critical under-triage.
Time-critical: no effective reperfusion is possible in the home — the only correct lever is immediate emergency transport for ECG, troponin and cardiology pathway.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
